description: Stage 2 frontend reconciler for projects generated by agentNewSolution2. V1 is autonomous and create-only: it scans l5 todoFrontend for owners with status = toCreate (l4 is read-only), generates the final L2 .defs.ts frontend artifacts, materializes the generated L2 .ts/.html files, and signs the client-owned l5/project.json (masters.frontend). The workspace config.json is composed at publish time by nodejsSaveConfigJson.ts.

entry.noInputPolicy: The agent receives no module, owner, page or device arguments. It discovers all actionable work from l5 todoFrontend; l4 is read-only.
entry.noWorkBehavior: If no owner with todoFrontend status = toCreate is found, finish without changing files or statuses.

slashCommands.@@changeFrontend: Alias for @@changeFrontend /run. Scan l4 and materialize stale/missing .ts files without resetting done owners.
slashCommands.@@changeFrontend /run: Scan l4 and materialize stale/missing .ts files without resetting done owners.
slashCommands.@@changeFrontend /rebuild all: Reset done owners to toCreate, regenerate .defs.ts and materialize stale/missing .ts files.
slashCommands.@@changeFrontend /rebuild defs: Reset done owners to toCreate and regenerate only .defs.ts. Do not materialize .ts.
slashCommands.@@changeFrontend /help: Return CLI-style help and finish the task.

source.stage1Contract: l4 is BUSINESS. agentNewSolution2 produces ontology, workflows, operations, rules, actors and process metadata only. It does not produce pages or per-page bffCommands.
source.schemaPattern: _102020_/l2/agentNewSolution2/ns2Schemas.ts + ns2Extract.ts (strict JSON-schema tool output enforced by collab-llm and locally revalidated)

createV1ImplementedNow: agentChangeFrontend now starts the create-only real flow instead of the v0.1 console flow.
agentCfeCreateScanL4 reads l4 owners (read-only) and their l5 todoFrontend status = toCreate and creates a real parallel fan-out with progress.

agentCfeCreatePage generates web/contracts deterministically, calls an LLM with a strict tool schema to generate web/desktop/page11 semantic layout defs for one page, reconciles layout state references, then writes web/shared from contractRef + layout-derived states/actions.
agentCfeCreatePage writes l2/{module}/trace/frontend-create-pages/{page}.json as inProgress/done so finalize does not accept stale page defs from an older run.

agentCfeMaterializeL2 reads the generated .defs.ts pipelines and creates sequential phase launchers for contracts, shared and pages. Each agentCfeMaterializePhase starts one progress fan-out only after the previous fan-out completed successfully, then schedules agentCfeMaterializeGen for .ts generation.

agentCfeMaterializeGen fails one Studio item explicitly on missing tool output or compile/typecheck errors, with the compiler details in trace. It writes companion .test.ts typecheck files using the MLS .test.ts extension slot. It does not re-emit prompt_ready from afterPromptStep because continue/recovery can orphan the original beforePromptStep hook.
agentCfeRegisterFrontend writes page preview .html files, signs l5/project.json (masters.frontend) and writes frontend-register-pages markers.
agentCfeCreateFinalize writes a trace report and marks generated owners todoFrontend status = done only after pages have create and register markers done.

The page11 layout keeps the section -> organism structure and enriches it with stable ids, intentions, field/column/action references and data bindings. i18n values live only in shared defs; page11 references keys. page11 is the plain implementation and does not reference molecule groups/tags.
v0.1Test: Implemented now only to test the autonomous l4 scan and page parallelism with children.
Reads l4 workflows/operations/modules/ontology and computes candidate pages.

Creates one parallel_dynamic fan-out parent with executionMode { type: 'parallel' }, so the runtime adds progress. Each dynamic page item then creates child steps contract -> layout -> shared/state reconciliation -> config.
Each page/container/child logs to console what would be created.
Does not write .defs.ts, config.json, .ts, .html or todoFrontend status.
v1: Scan l4 owners (read-only) and process only those with l5 todoFrontend status = toCreate.
Exit cleanly when there is no create work.
Generate final L2 .defs.ts files directly under web/contracts, web/shared and web/desktop/page11.

Preserve only frontend-facing field metadata in contract input/output fields, including enum, description and statusEnum-derived enum. Do not emit backend/L4 provenance such as sourceEntity/sourceField, lifecycleStates, table refs, usecase refs, layer contracts or rulesApplied.
Sign l5/project.json with the frontend master signature; the workspace config.json (pages/menu) is composed at publish time from l4 + materialized l2 artifacts.
Generate a semantic page layout in the page .defs.ts using the layout rules from spec.md.
Use JSON-schema-backed LLM outputs whenever an LLM is used, following the agentNewSolution2 pattern.
Run page generation in parallel by page when the runtime supports the planned shape.
Mark processed owners todoFrontend status = done after all required .defs.ts files validate.
explicitlyNotInV1: No old top-level l2/{module}/{page}.defs.ts general page source file.
deferredToV2: toUpdate diff and preservation of manual edits.
patching existing pages created by a previous run.
frontend/backend reconciliation beyond shared action ids. Backend resolves operation/usecase/table/rule metadata directly from l4.
notResponsible: Changing ontology, workflows, operations, rules or actors. l4 is read-only; generation status lives in l5 todoFrontend.

recommendedFor: fallback when schema-valid output still misses frontend intent
cheap fallback before escalating to codepro/codeinstruct

researchBasis: agentNewSolution2 uses codepro for schema-bound definition agents such as agentNs2EntityDefinition, agentNs2WorkflowDefinition and agentPlanOperationDefinition.
agentNewSolution2 uses codefast for the root orchestration prompt.
agentNewSolution2 uses codeinstruct for heavier reference/planning branches such as plugins, MDM, horizontals and fan-out queue prompts.
Local materialization agents use stronger code models for concrete code generation; this flow uses agentCfeMaterializeGen after .defs.ts creation.
modelSelection.defaultPolicy: Use no LLM for deterministic scan, status mutation and validation. When an LLM is required, force strict JSON-schema tool output and local validation.

orchestration.reason: Small routing/orchestration prompt; no domain generation.

pagePlanning.reason: Closest local precedent is agentNewSolution2 definition/index agents, which use codepro for structured schema output.

contractDefs.reason: Maps l4 operations/workflows and ontology into typed bffCommands; structured, but lower visual ambiguity than layout.

sharedDefs.reason: Builds actions/state/navigation from contract commands with strict references.

layoutDefs.reason: The page layout is user-facing and important; use the strongest code/layout model for the semantic layout tree.

repair.reason: Use cheap repair for small schema failures; escalate only when reference mapping is wrong.

summary.reason: Summary is low-risk and can be skipped if deterministic reporting is enough.

schemaFirst.collabLlmValidation: Follow the agentNewSolution2 pattern: each LLM agent exposes one JSON-schema-backed tool; collab-llm validates tool arguments, then the local agent validates again before saving files.
schemaFirst.validationPolicy: Invalid schema output may be retried once. If still invalid, block the affected page and do not mark its owners done.

CfePageIndexResult.purpose: Group selected l4 owners into pages, with pageId, moduleName, actorIds, ownerIds, sections, organisms, menu/navigation metadata and page genome.

CfeContractDefsResult.purpose: Contract .defs.ts content with pure frontend-facing BFF commands: commandName, purpose, kind, input and output shapes only.

CfeSharedDefsResult.purpose: Shared .defs.ts content with contractRef/layoutRef, actions, states, initialLoads, navigationRefs, i18n keys and automation metadata. It must not mirror bffCommands from the contract.

CfeLayoutDefsResult.purpose: Page/layout .defs.ts content with semantic layout tree, stable ids, sections, organisms, plain page11 intentions, bindings and display hints.

CfePageDefsValidationResult.purpose: Deterministic validation report for one generated page.

CfeContractSharedLayoutDefsResult.purpose: Fallback schema for a single per-page agent that generates contract, shared, layout and page validation when nested dynamic children are not supported.

ownerStatus.failureStatus: leave as inProgress and write a trace record; v1 has no failed enum

conventions.idSpace: All entity references in pages and bffCommands use canonical ontology ids from l4. Never use aggregate/group names.
conventions.pageGrouping: An LLM may group multiple operations/workflows into one page when their stories/capabilities/actors naturally compose. A page contains sections and organisms; organisms can map to one or more operations.

pageGenome.meaning: page11 is the simple/default page genome. Other genomes are handled by later agents.

conventions.generatedTypecheckFiles: Materialization may write companion .test.ts files beside generated .ts files. In MLS storage this is extension .test.ts with the same base shortName as the generated .ts; do not parse createStockItem.test.ts as shortName=createStockItem.test plus extension=.ts. Studio materialization must save local content before creating/compiling the model, because generated .test.ts files do not exist in the remote driver during rebuild.
conventions.pipeline: Generated .defs.ts files must include pipeline metadata executed by agentCfeMaterializeL2 through agentCfeMaterializeGen after .defs.ts creation.
conventions.rulesHandling: Do not emit rulesApplied in web/contracts. Backend-facing rule metadata stays in l4 and is read directly by agentChangeBackend. Page/shared metadata may reference rules only when needed for frontend behavior and must not block page creation.
conventions.layoutContract: The page .defs.ts contains semantic layout, not raw DOM/CSS. Layout nodes use stable id/order keys and reference shared actions/state/contracts/i18n keys by key only; i18n values live in shared defs. It is later materialized into plain Lit/render code by agentChangeFrontend/skills/genCfePage11RenderTs.ts. The full section -> organism -> intention tree lives in definition.layout.sections; top-level sections is only a compact compatibility summary. page11 must not reference molecule groups, molecule tags or package-specific component names.

parallelStrategy.target: Generate different pages in parallel. Inside each page, generate contract -> layout -> shared/state reconciliation -> validation as a child sequence.
confirmedFromExistingFlows: parallel_dynamic fan-out exists and is used by agentNewSolution2 for entities, workflows and operations.
per-page chained parallel_static steps with an ALL-pages barrier exist in agentImplementGenome.
parallelStrategy.notYetConfirmed: No existing flow was found that proves a parallel_dynamic item can itself own a multi-agent child sequence. Treat nested page children as the target design, not yet a runtime guarantee.
parallelStrategy.implementationRule: If nested children are implemented, child steps must not depend on a passive container that waits for its children; they should depend on the same upstream page index or on the previous child step, following the agentNewSolution2 deadlock rule.
parallelStrategy.fallback: If the runtime rejects nested children under parallel_dynamic, replace the child sequence with one parallel_dynamic agent generate-page-defs:<page> that performs contract, shared, layout and per-page validation internally, while keeping validate-all-defs as the barrier.

materializationRecovery.studioPolicy: Do not retry by returning prompt_ready from agentCfeMaterializeGen.afterPromptStep. On missing tool output or compile/typecheck errors, fail the affected item with a trace message and let the phase/fan-out report the failure.
materializationRecovery.reason: A 102048 run showed a continue/recovery failure during shared materialization repair: the retry attempted to locate a beforePromptStep for the fan-out parent and failed with No matching beforePromptStep found. Failing explicitly is safer than leaving the task in waiting_after_prompt.
materializationRecovery.cliPolicy: The Node CLI can retry safely because it owns the prompt loop and filesystem context locally. It may retry missing/truncated LLM output and run one tsc repair round with compiler errors in the repair prompt. The CLI checks generated artifacts through a temporary scoped tsconfig with noImplicitAny and strictNullChecks enabled, so unrelated strict errors in other base projects do not block materialization repair. collab-llm HTTP/request failures, including timeouts, are item failures and are not sent to the repair loop: record the trace, continue the remaining items, print a final failure summary and exit nonzero if any item failed.

loggingPolicy.studioConsole: Studio agents must not use console.log for progress. Progress belongs in task trace/progress titles. Only console.warn and console.error are allowed for real diagnostics.
loggingPolicy.taskTrace: Every failed materialization update-status must include the same diagnostic text sent to console.error. Warnings produced during create/materialize are appended to traceMsg on the affected step.
loggingPolicy.typecheckDiagnostics: TypeScript diagnostics from generated .test.ts files are formatted as readable TS errors before being written to traceMsg; raw diagnostic JSON should not be used as the user-facing error.

writes: status on processed l5 todoFrontend owners (l4 read-only)

steps.goal: Root orchestrator. No input args. Start an autonomous scan for create work and spawn the defs-only create chain. If the scan finds no work, finish immediately.
Deterministically read l4/l5 and select all owners with l5 todoFrontend status = toCreate. Owners in toUpdate/toRemove/inProgress/done are reported but not processed in v1.
steps.output: A create change-set: moduleName, selected operations, selected workflows, current status values, source file refs.
steps.ifEmpty: Complete the flow with no file/status writes.

steps.goal: Deterministic preflight. Validate selected owners only: operationIds referenced by selected workflows exist, entities/fields/rules resolve, actors exist, page11 genome can be targeted, and no selected owner is already in progress/done.
steps.output: Validated create change-set plus warnings for non-blocking issues such as materializer rulesPath shape.

steps.goal: Deterministically set l5 todoFrontend status = inProgress for the validated toCreate owners. This is the only status mutation before successful completion.
steps.updates: l5 todoFrontend owners for selected owners

steps.goal: LLM-assisted page planning. Group selected workflows/operations into pages, choose pageId/pageName/actor/purpose/menu label/navigation metadata, assign owners to page sections/organisms, define command outlines, and choose page genome web/desktop/page11.
constraints: Create only. Do not modify l4 business artifacts except statuses already locked.
Do not copy domain names from examples; derive from target l4.
Do not create the old l2/{module}/{page}.defs.ts general source file.
Reuse canonical pageId, commandName and bffName from l4 workflows/operations when present.
Use page11 for every page in v1.
Keep organisms semantic and layout ids stable.
Save navigation/menu intent in defs; the publish-time composer derives page/menu entries from l4 + materialized l2.

steps.goal: Per-page fan-out container. Target design: one page per parallel item, with child agents for contract, layout, shared/state reconciliation and validation.
steps.fallback: If nested children are not supported, run generate-page-defs:<page> as a single parallel_dynamic child that performs all four page tasks internally.

steps.goal: For one planned page, generate web/contracts/{page}.defs.ts. Resolve bffCommands from l4 owners and ontology.
steps.definitionRequirements: definition is an array of pure BFF commands with commandName, bffName when available from L4, routeKey, purpose, kind, input, output and origin. origin is only a lightweight L4 owner reference (operation/workflow id + defPath + bffName when available). Do not include readsEntities, writesEntities, table refs, usecaseRefs, layerContract, rulesApplied or field-level source provenance.

steps.goal: For one planned page, generate web/shared/{page}.defs.ts after page11 exists. Reference the contract and layout, include contract-derived states/actions, add layout-required supplemental shared states, and do not duplicate bffCommands.
steps.definitionRequirements: definition is { pageId, pageName, moduleName, origin, contractRef, layoutRef, states, actions, initialLoads, navigationRefs, i18nMeta, i18n, automation }. It references the contract commands by commandRef, must not duplicate bffCommands, and every page11 stateKey/action reference must resolve to shared.states/shared.actions.

steps.goal: For one planned page, generate web/desktop/page11/{page}.defs.ts with a useful semantic layout following spec.md. The layout has access to the contract and base shared state keys; missing UI state is reconciled into shared after layout generation.
steps.definitionRequirements: definition contains page metadata, navigationRefs, sections/organisms and semantic layout with stable ids/order/action/state/i18n key references. It must not duplicate i18n values from shared.

steps.goal: Deterministically validate the three generated .defs.ts files for one page: export shape, pipeline ids/types/paths, dependsFiles, no old general page defs, output paths under web/desktop/page11, references to known owner ids/entities/rules/actions, and all page11 state/action refs declared in shared.

Barrier after page fan-out. Validate cross-page uniqueness and consistency: pageIds, command names, navigation refs, owner coverage, no duplicate owners assigned to incompatible pages, and all required .defs.ts files present.
Deterministically sign the client-owned l5/project.json with masters.frontend (masterProject, agentFolder, runtimeProject) so the publish resolves the frontend composer. Does not write config.json.
Set l5 todoFrontend status = done for all selected owners whose required .defs.ts files were generated and validated. Leave unverified owners in inProgress with trace details.
steps.updates: l5 todoFrontend owners for selected owners

steps.goal: Produce a concise final report: pages planned, .defs.ts files created, owner ids marked done, warnings, and v2-deferred owners.

v0.1ImplementedAgents.role: Reads l4, derives candidate pages, creates parallel page containers and final barrier.
Parallel per-page container. Logs the page that would be created.
Barrier after all config child logs.

createV1ImplementedAgents.role: Reads l4, finds l5 todoFrontend=toCreate owners, creates create-page-fanout with progress, then schedules materialization.
For one page, writes contract deterministically, asks the LLM for schema-validated page11 layout, writes shared from the saved layout, validates references, and writes the page .defs.ts.
Reads generated page .defs.ts pipelines, creates sequential contract/shared/page phase launchers, then schedules frontend registration after the last materialization fan-out.
Starts one parallel materialization fan-out only after its dependency barrier has completed.

Generates one frontend L2 .ts file from a generated .defs.ts pipeline item using local agentChangeFrontend materialization helpers, then saves the companion .test.ts typecheck file with the MLS .test.ts extension slot. In Studio it fails explicitly on missing output or compile/typecheck errors instead of retrying in the same hook.
Generates page .html previews, signs l5/project.json (masters.frontend) and marks page registration done.
Saves frontend-create-report.json and marks processed owners done after materialization and registration.
Shared L4 reader, contract/shared defs writer, layout schema/validator, page defs writer, config merger and status updater.

fallbackFlatPageStep.goal: Fallback for runtimes that cannot run a dynamic page item with child agents. For one page, generate contract, shared, layout and page validation internally, saving the same artifacts.

produces: Final L2 frontend .defs.ts with pipeline metadata
Navigation/menu intent in shared/page defs and trace
todoFrontend status done on processed owners
consumedBy: agentImplementGenome for alternate page genomes/design systems and molecule-based implementations such as page12

failurePolicy.beforeLock: Fail without changing l4 owner statuses.
failurePolicy.afterLock: Do not mark done. Keep affected owners in inProgress and write trace details, because the current status enum has no failed state.
failurePolicy.partialPageFailure: A page failure blocks done for owners assigned to that page only; unrelated pages may finish if their validation succeeds.
failurePolicy.noWork: No selected owners means no status or file writes.
failurePolicy.manualRecovery: A later recovery tool or v2 flow may reset stale inProgress owners to toCreate after inspection.
